Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

It's odd to be so hard on my own patch, but I think this patch is wrong.  It's a hack - which Frank Zago thinks isn't even fully correct - to avoid proper lifecycle management of the shared iobuf.  I don't think this should ever land, it's just here to point out the problem of sharing a thread specific object between threads.  (And because it allows Cray to avoid a bug that impaired our testing.)

commit.subject: LU-6654 osd: Add osd_iobuf concurrency control
commit.message:
LU-6654 osd: Add osd_iobuf concurrency control

The osd_iobuf in osd_thread_info is used by a thread
other than the one which owns the osd_thread_info.

The existing concurrency control (checking dr_numreqs) has
a race condition where dr_numreqs is decremented, which
opens the path for the other thread to eventually free
osd_thread_info, and then the first thread calls wakeup
on a wait queue which is inside the now freed osd_thread_info.

This patch changed the "osd_iobuf" in "osd_thread_info" to
an independent allocated memory protected by references.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <paf@cray.com>
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
Change-Id: If9b2f66e69e227d00a84660c66b856d29c7ab323
